zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir George Barley I於1270年出生在英國英格蘭德比夏爾時，他的父親Sir John Barley30歲，母親Debarley de Vernon26歲。他於1299年與Lady Ursula Curzon在英國英格蘭德比夏爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1330年在他的家鄉享年60歲。

nameMeanings.name: Barley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Barley. Those in Lancashire and Yorkshire are named with Old English bār ‘wild boar’ or bere ‘barley’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’. A place of the same name in Hertfordshire has as its first element an unattested Old English byname Be(o)ra (from bera ‘bear’).

English: metonymic occupational name for a grower or seller of barley, from Middle English barli, barlich ‘barley’ (Old English bærlic, originally an adjective derivative of bær ‘barley’, a byform of bere).
Americanized form of South German Behrle or of its Swiss German cognates Beerli, Berli, or Berly, and probably also of some other similar (like-sounding) surname. Compare Berley .

variants.name: Behrle
meanings: South German (Württemberg): from a pet form of Behr ‘bear’, including from a pet form of an ancient Germanic personal name formed with Old High German bero ‘bear’.
Altered form of Norwegian Berle: habitational name from a farmstead in the county of Sogn og Fjordane, so named with Old Norse barth ‘rim, edge’.

nameMeanings.name: George
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir George Barley I was born in 1270, in Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir John Barley, was 30 and his mother, Debarley de Vernon, was 26. He married Lady Ursula Curzon in 1299, in Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. He died in 1330, in his hometown, at the age of 60.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir George Barley I(이)가 1270년 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Barley(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Debarley de Vernon(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1299년 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르에서 Lady Ursula Curzon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1330년 그의 고향에서 60세의 나이로 사망했다.
